pi_items.source: Task description says 'You are an interior designer preparing a renovation proposal for a client walkthrough.' This is standard interior design / renovation consulting work.
Sweet Home 3D is open-source interior design software (sweethome3d.com). The task — creating a presentation-quality renovation from a building shell — is a core interior design workflow.
verifier.py line 176: 'passed = score >= 65'. Cross-checked by running verifier against clean starter state — returned score=0 with wrong-target gate as expected.
verifier.py: C1=20 + C2=20 + C3=15 + C4=25 + C5=20 = 100. Verified by reading every scoring path in the verifier code.

Live test confirmed: setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/Documents/SweetHome3D/apartment_renovation_starter.sh3d (1,774,757 bytes, MD5=62a4761cbe8c130b5602fa212d318606) from SweetHome3DExample.sh3d. File exists and opens in SH3D correctly — verified via screenshot.
Live test: baseline JSON shows wall_count=16. XML analysis confirms 16 wall elements in the stripped starter. These are the structural apartment walls the agent works within.

Live test: baseline JSON shows room_count=6. The apartment has 6 room polygons (unnamed, no floor textures). Sizes: 3.7m2, 9.2m2, 23.2m2, 5.3m2, 11.8m2, 7.9m2. The task asks to name 5 of these 6 rooms (the 6th is likely a hallway/corridor).
Live test: baseline JSON confirms furniture_count=0. Setup script strips ALL pieceOfFurniture, furnitureGroup, and doorOrWindow elements. XML analysis confirms 0 furniture items.

Live test: XML analysis shows walls_with_texture=0. Setup script strips leftSideColor, rightSideColor, leftSideShininess, rightSideShininess attributes, and leftSideTexture, rightSideTexture, leftSideBaseboard, rightSideBaseboard child elements from all walls.

Task says 'Define and name 5 rooms: Living Room, Kitchen, Bedroom, Bathroom, and Home Office.' Verifier C1 checks named_rooms >= 5. Room naming dialog confirmed accessible via right-click > Modify rooms (screenshot evidence: 07b_modify_room_dialog.png).

Task says 'Apply a distinct floor texture or color to each room.' Verifier C1 checks rooms_with_floor_color >= 5. Export script detects floorColor attribute and floorTexture child elements on room XML. Modify Rooms dialog has floor color/texture options (screenshot evidence: 07b_modify_room_dialog.png).

Task says 'Apply wall colors or textures to walls in at least 3 rooms.' Verifier C2 checks walls_with_texture >= 8 (approx 3 rooms x 3 walls each). Export script detects leftSideColor/rightSideColor attrs and leftSideTexture/rightSideTexture children. Modify Walls dialog confirmed accessible via right-click (screenshot evidence: 07c_modify_wall_dialog.png) with left/right side color and texture options visible.

Task says 'Install at least 5 interior doors from the Doors and windows catalog.' Verifier C3 checks door_window_count >= 5. Export script counts items with doorOrWindow='true'. Catalog confirmed to have: Door, Open door, Door frame, Front door, French window, Sliding doors, Rounded door (screenshot evidence: 07a_doors_catalog.png).

Task says 'placing at least 30 items total.' Verifier C4 checks furniture_count >= 30 and categories_with_items >= 5. All required items verified in SH3D built-in catalog: Bathroom (Bath, Toilet unit, Washbasin), Bedroom (Bed 140x190, Bedside table, Wardrobe), Kitchen (Cooker, Fridge), Living room (Sofa, Armchair, Round table, TV unit, Bookcase, Chair), Lights (8 types), and Doors (7 types). Evidence: 12_required_items_availability.txt.

Task says 'At least 3 items must be wall-mounted at a specific elevation (e.g., set shelf elevation to 120cm, TV unit to 80cm, or picture frame to 150cm) using the furniture modification dialog.' Verifier C4 checks elevated_items >= 3 where elevation > 20cm. Export script reads float(elem.get('elevation', '0')). Furniture modification dialog is accessible via double-click on any placed item (standard SH3D functionality, confirmed in tv_news_studio_layout run_3 trajectory).

Task says 'Add at least 4 dimension lines documenting key room measurements.' Verifier C5 checks new_dimensions >= 4. Export script counts dimensionLine XML elements. Create dimensions tool accessible via Plan menu (screenshot evidence: 07e_plan_menu.png).

Task says 'Create a 3D photo rendering... save it to ~/Desktop/renovation_render.png.' Verifier C5 checks photo_found and photo_size > 10000. Export script searches for PNG/JPG files in ~/Desktop created after task start. 3D View > Create photo menu confirmed accessible (screenshot evidence: 07d_3dview_menu.png). Export script searches multiple paths: /home/ga/Desktop/renovation_render.png, /home/ga/Desktop/*.png, /home/ga/Desktop/*.jpg, etc.

Task description explicitly lists 5 rooms: Living Room, Kitchen, Bedroom, Bathroom, Home Office. Verifier C1 gives full credit at named_rooms >= 5. The starter shell has 6 room polygons so 5 can be named (the 6th is likely a hallway or corridor, area 3.7m2).
Task says 'Apply a distinct floor texture or color to each room' (5 rooms). Verifier C1 gives full credit at rooms_with_floor_color >= 5.

Task says 'at least 3 rooms'. Verifier C2 checks walls_with_texture >= 8 for full credit. With 16 total walls across 6 rooms, approximately 2-3 walls per room, texturing 3 rooms = 6-9 wall segments. Threshold of 8 is consistent with 3 rooms at ~3 walls each.
Task description: 'Install at least 5 interior doors.' Verifier C3 gives full credit at door_window_count >= 5.
Task description: 'placing at least 30 items total'. Verifier C4 gives full volume points at furniture_count >= 30 AND categories_with_items >= 5.

Task requires items from at least 5 catalog category groups: beds (bedroom), bath_fixtures (bathroom), kitchen_appliances, shelves/desks/sofas/tables/chairs (living room), and lamps (lights). Verifier C4 checks categories_with_items >= 5 (excluding doors from category count).

Task description: 'At least 3 items must be wall-mounted at a specific elevation.' Verifier C4 checks elevated_items >= 3 where elevation > 20cm. The examples given are shelf at 120cm, TV unit at 80cm, picture frame at 150cm — all well above the 20cm detection threshold.
Task description: 'Add at least 4 dimension lines.' Verifier C5 gives full dimension credit at new_dimensions >= 4.
Task description: 'Create a 3D photo rendering... save it to ~/Desktop/renovation_render.png.' Verifier C5 gives 8 points for photo_found=true and photo_size > 10000 bytes.

Task description: 'Save the project as apartment_renovation_final.sh3d via File > Save As.' Verifier checks file_changed (MD5 differs from baseline). Export script also checks for this specific filename at /home/ga/Documents/SweetHome3D/apartment_renovation_final.sh3d.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a stripped Sweet Home 3D sample file (SweetHome3DExample.sh3d) serving as a bare apartment shell. The starter contains 16 wall segments and 6 unnamed room polygons with areas of 3.7m2, 9.2m2, 23.2m2, 5.3m2, 11.8m2, and 7.9m2. All furniture (including doors/windows), room names, floor textures/colors, wall textures/colors, labels, and dimension lines have been removed. The baseline MD5 is 62a4761cbe8c130b5602fa212d318606. The agent must name 5 of the 6 room polygons (Living Room, Kitchen, Bedroom, Bathroom, Home Office), apply floor textures to all 5, and apply wall textures to walls in at least 3 rooms (verifier requires >= 8 wall segments with leftSideColor/rightSideColor or leftSideTexture/rightSideTexture attributes). Wall textures are set via right-click wall > 'Modify walls...' dialog which has left/right side color and texture pickers. The agent must place >= 5 doors from the 'Doors and windows' catalog (available items: Door, Open door, Door frame, Front door, Rounded door, Sliding doors, French window), place >= 30 furniture items spanning >= 5 categories (beds, bath_fixtures, kitchen_appliances, shelves, sofas, tables, chairs, desks, lamps, decor), set elevation > 20cm on >= 3 items using the furniture modification dialog's Elevation field, add >= 4 dimension lines, create a 3D photo rendering (via 3D View > Create photo, must be > 10KB) saved to ~/Desktop/renovation_render.png, and save the project as apartment_renovation_final.sh3d. All required items are confirmed available in the SH3D built-in catalog. Scoring: C1(20)=rooms+floors, C2(20)=wall finishes, C3(15)=doors, C4(25)=furniture+elevation, C5(20)=render+dims+save. Pass threshold: 65/100.
